vector_chunks.text: Lisätään sakkaan 20 ml (2x tilavuus) NaCl-puskuria ja homogenoidaan Ultra Turraxilla

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Siirrä sakka SS-34 putkesta punakorkkiseen purkkiin, GSA:ssa voi homogenisoida suoraan.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Kiinnitä terä laitteeseen niin, että kolmio on edessä ja hios ei näy.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Homogenisoi jäillä, n. 11 teholla 2x 1 min.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Terän voi pestä ster H2O, säilyttää EtOH y/y ja pestä seuraavana päivänä urealla ennen käyttöä.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Viimeisen käytön jälkeen pese Virkonissa, avaa terä, pese myös sisältä ja autoklavoi. Pese kaikki välineet ensin Virkonissa.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Fuugataan 7000 rpm 20 min ja poistetaan suppi.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Toistetaan vaiheet 2 ja 3.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Lisää sakkaan 10 ml (sama määrä kuin sakkaa oli g) 2 M Urea-puskuria, pH 7.4. Sekoita ja kaada punakorkkiseen purkkiin. Homogenoi kudoshomogenisaattorilla jäillä.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Lisää purkkiin steriili magneetti ja sekoita yön yli +4 oC:ssa.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: (Päivä 2 tai 3, varaa n. 9 h)

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Laita seos sentrifuugiputkeen ja sentrifugoi 14 000 rpm 20 min +4 oC. Ota supernatantti talteen puhtaaseen sentrifuugiputkeen. (Supin päällä voi rasvaa, jota kannattaa välttää)

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Laita sekoittumaan 1000 ml TBS + 5,6 ml kloroformia mittalasiin.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Lisää 5 ml (puolet sakan määrästä) 2M Urea-puskuria sakkaputkeen ja sekoita. Kaada punakorkkiseen purkkiin ja homogenisoi kudoshomogenisaattorilla jäillä.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Siirrä seos sentrifuugiputkeen ja fuugaa 14 000 rpm 20 min +4oC. Yhdistä supernatantit.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Sentifugoi yhdistetyt supernatantit 14000 rpm 20 min +4 oC.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Huuhtele dialyysipussit (MWCO 8 kDa) steriilillä vedellä sentrifugoinnin aikana noin 30 min ennen käyttöä. (n. 35 cm on riittävä 12 ml:lle liuosta) Tee pussiin solmu ja laita sen yläpuolelle klipsi.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Pipetoi supernatantti puhtaaseen falconiin ja sieltä dialyysipussiin ja sulje pussi klipsillä. Jätä tarpeeksi tyhjää tilaa, kun täytät pussin.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Laita dialyysipussit mittalasiin, jossa sekoitettu 1000 ml TBS + 5,6 ml kloroformia. Anna sekoittua magneettisekoittajalla +4 oC:ssa ainakin 2 h. Jos on suurempi erä käytä 2 l TBS:ää ja/tai vaihda 1 h välein.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Laita uuteen mittalasiin 1 l TBS:ää ja siirrä dialyysipussit tähän. Anna sekoittua +4 oC:ssa ainakin kaksi tuntia.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Vaihda uusi TBS ja anna sekoittua +4 oC:ssa ainakin 2 h.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Laita uuteen mittalasiin 1 litra seerumitonta mediumia (DMEM/F-12). Siirrä dialyysipussit tähän. Anna olla sekoituksessa yön yli +4 oC:ssa.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: (päivä 3 tai 4)

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Siirrä geeli sentrifuugiputkeen, sentrifugoi 14 000 rpm 20 min +4 oC ja kerää geelisuppi talteen puhtaaseen Falconiin.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Ota proteiinin mittausta varten 50 µl syrjään ja jaa geeli sopiviin eriin (esimerkiksi 300 µl – 10 ml) steriilisti laminaarissa. Säilytä geelit-20 oC:ssa.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Myogeelin valmistus.docx
vector_chunks.text: Tee proteiininmääritys 1:2, 1:5 ja 1:10 laimennoksesta.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Omentum gel making.docx
vector_chunks.text: Omentum gel making

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Omentum gel making.docx
vector_chunks.text: Make a modification a myogel protocol, omit the NaCl wash and start directly with the addition of the urea buffer.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Omentum gel making.docx
vector_chunks.text: 1. Omentum Crushed with Liquid Nitrogen Crusher 50 g (in Red-Capped Jars)

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Omentum gel making.docx
vector_chunks.text: 2. Add 50 ml 2M urea buffer pH 7.4 to the precipitate and homogenize with ultra-turrax.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Omentum gel making.docx
vector_chunks.text: 3. Place overnight with stirring at + 4°C.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Omentum gel making.docx
vector_chunks.text: 4. Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm (tubes with a cap) with Beckman JA-30-50 or SS-34 rotor for 20 min. Recover the supernatant.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Omentum gel making.docx
vector_chunks.text: 5. Add about 25 ml or less of 2M urea to the centrifugation tubes and homogenize again with ultrasound.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Omentum gel making.docx
vector_chunks.text: 6. Repeat step 4 and combine the supernatants. Centrifuge once more to properly remove the precipitate.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Omentum gel making.docx
vector_chunks.text: 7. Place the supernatant in the dialysis bag MWCO 8000 Da (Spectra/Por 7 Dialysis Tubing, 8 kD MWCO, 24 mm Flat-width, 5 meters/roll, #132114) (bags washed with Q dH2O before use).

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Omentum gel making.docx
vector_chunks.text: 8. First dialyze with 900 ml TBS + 5 ml chloroform with stirring at + 4 ° C for 2h

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Omentum gel making.docx
vector_chunks.text: 9. Replace the bags in a clean container and dialyze a further 2x with TBS at least 2h, switch to serum-free DMEM / F-12, and dialyze overnight.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Omentum gel preparations (protocols from Salo)/Omentum gel making.docx
vector_chunks.text: 10. Collect gels and centrifuge for an additional 14,000 rpm for 20 min. Sterile divide into 300 µl and 500 µl aliquots and store at -20°C, which does not thaw itself.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LITERATURE REVIEW FOR ATXi (GLPG1690)/PERKi (AMG PERK 44) OPTIMIZATION PLAN

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: Matilda Salko 07/2021

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: Elina – tästä meitä kiinnostavasta GLPG1690:sta ei löytynyt juurikaan in vitro -tutkimuksia PubMedistä. PERKi-inhibiittori AMG PERK 44:n suhteen vielä heikommat löydökset (haasteena on tietysti saattanut olla myös etsijän tiedonhakutaidot…). Tässä alussa on vähän muistiinpanoja taustasta ja LPA-pathwaysta ylipäätään, lähinnä mulle itselleni opetukseksi. Alempana ne edes suhteellisen relevantilta vaikuttavat artikkelit, joita löysin.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: Information from Juan R Cubillos-Ruiz: “These are the conditions that we would suggest testing in your system to determine if they are able to modulate (hopefully improve) response to IO + PARPi:

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: Autotaxin inhibitor GLPG1690 used at final concentrations ranging from 0.2 - 1 uM

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: PERK inhibitor AMG PERK 44 used at final concentrations ranging from 0.2 - 1 uM

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: Combination of 1 and 2. above “

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LPA (lysophosphatidic acid) in tumors

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LPA is a water-soluble phospholipid, which, by current knowledge, acts through 6 g protein-coupled receptors: LPAR1-LPAR6.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: In addition GPR87 (g protein-coupled receptor 87) and (orphan) P2Y10

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: TRPV1 (transient receptor potential vanilloid 1) responds to LPA stimuli, in addition to other protein-mediated stimuli.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: Produced during cell membrane synthesis and acts as a robust extracellular signaling molecule present in tissues and blood plasma.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LPA-mediated signaling is responsible for proliferation, migration, and cytoskeletal reorganization, to name a few.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LPA is present in all eukaryotic tissues and fluids, both in physiological and pathological conditions

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LPA can be present both intra- and extracellularly

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: One major pathway involved in LPA extracellular synthesis, where lysophospholipids (LPs, which are produced from membrane phospholipid precursors), are converted into LPA by autotaxin (ATX; ENPP2).

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: In (solid) cancers, increased LPA signaling is related to numerous roles:

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LPAR1 and LPAR4 - mostly associated with cell motility and invasion

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LPAR4 is strongly expressed in ovaries under physiological conditions

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LPAR2 - cell survival and apoptosis inhibition

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LPAR3 and LPAR6 - promotion of tumor proliferation, migration, invasiveness, aggressiveness, and progression of different cancers

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: Also, LPAR3 has been related to the regulation of dendritic cell migration, and possibly having an antitumor immunity role

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LPAR5 - involved in immune response inhibition and cancer progression

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: ATX and LPA are released by both tumor cells and other cells in the TME, increasing invasion, suppressing antitumor response and contributing to therapy resistance.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LPA-LPAR signaling has also been demonstrated to promote EMT

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LPA has also been shown to induce the production of proinflammatory cytokines, such as IL6 and IL8 - impacting the progression and metastasis. An inflammatory environment creates a positive loop where LPA-LPAR signaling is enhanced

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: Increased LPA signaling has been linked to carcinogenesis and tumor progression as well as reprogramming of metastatic microenvironments and development of therapy resistance

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: One study taken into account in this review reported that chemotherapy inhibits expression of LPAR1-3

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: In addition, there is a positive loop in which the inflammatory milieu induces more LPA-LPAR signaling and chronic inflammation can lead to malignant transformation.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LPA receptors and signaling intermediates. LPA signals through six different LPA transmembrane G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs and LPAR1–6) and other GPCRs (such as P2Y10 and GRP87). These receptors activate different intracellular signaling mediators to elicit different/several cellular responses (such as cytoskeleton reorganization, migration, proliferation, survival, and cell–cell communication). Finally, LPA was also recently described as a direct PPARγ agonist and in this sense intracellular LPA could also induce signaling through this receptor. Scheme created using Biorender.com

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: In ovarian cancer:

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: “LPA has a tremendously described influence on ovarian cancer. It acts on the EMT, glucose metabolism, cell migration, among other roles; and also functions as a biomarker.” (ref PMID: 33526777).

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: In ovarian cancer, LPA affects the EMT, glucose metabolism, cell migration, and other factors

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LPA-promoted glycolytic shift could lead to transformation of normal fibroblasts to CAFs

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LPA Induces Metabolic Reprogramming in Ovarian Cancer via a Pseudohypoxic Response (2018, PMID: 29386184)

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: Study used patient-derived ovarian cancer cells (isolated from ascites samples), and HGSOC cell lines (ex. Kuramochi, OVCAR8, SKOV3-ip among others) to demonstrate the effect of LPA signaling on metabolism of OC cells

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LPA induced an increase in aerobic glycolysis and glycolytic capacity in all tested ovarian cancer cells

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: Ovarian Cancer Cell-derived Lysophosphatidic Acid Induces Glycolytic Shift and Cancer-associated Fibroblast-Phenotype in Normal and Peritumoral Fibroblasts (2019, PMID: 30503552)

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: Experiment done on immortalized normal ovarian fibroblasts and ovarian CAFs (CAF147, CAF148)

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: LPA induced CAF-associated phenotypic changes in normal ovarian fibroblasts, and glycolysis and glycolytic capacity in differentiated ovarian CAFs

vector_chunks.text: ATX function Zhang X, Li M, Yin N, Zhang J. The Expression Regulation and Biological Function of Autotaxin. Cells. 2021 Apr 19;10(4):939. doi: 10.3390/cells10040939. PMID: 33921676; PMCID: PMC8073485. (review)

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Literature review for ATXi-PERKi optimization plan.docx
vector_chunks.text: ATX-LPA-LPA receptor signaling axis and LPA delivery on the surface of target cell. LPA is mainly synthesized via the ATX-mediated hydrolysis of lysophosphatidylcholine (LPC) and degraded to monoacylglycerol (MAG) through LPP1/3. ATX protein is composed of two N-terminal somatomedin B (SMB)-like domains, a central phosphodiesterase (PDE) domain, and a C-terminal nuclease (NUC)-like domain. The SMB2 domain enables ATX to bind with cell surface integrin, which achieves a highly efficient delivery of LPA to its receptors. When it binds to LPAR1-6, LPA induces different downstream signal cascades, including Rho, phospholipase C (PLC), mitogen-activated protein kinase (MAPK), phosphatidylinositide 3-kinase (PI3K), and adenylate cyclase (AC), through different G proteins.

vector_chunks.text:
High-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (HGSC)

Most common (~70%) and lethal subtype of ovarian cancers
 ~ 80% of the patients exhibit omental metastasis


Frequent genetic alterations
homologous recombination deficiency (HRD) (~50%)
mutation in TPS3, BRCA1/2 genes (~20%)


Potential targeted therapies
PARPi effective against HRD tumors
immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICI) response rate is 8-10%
better response to combination of PARPi plus anti-PD-1 in HRD HGSC

Nieman K.M et al., 2011
Cojocaru et al., 2017
Färkkilä et al., 2020

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Presentations/GSK_STINGa_update_2023-09-12.pptx
vector_chunks.text:
Research objective:

To test STING agonist (GSK3859856) alone and in combinations with anti- PD-1 antibody, PARP inhibitor and other agents on patient-derived immunocompetent cultures (iPDCs) established from HGSC patient’s undergoing surgery at Helsinki University Hospital.

Readouts:
 a) Cytotoxic response from tumor and immune cells using image-based analysis 
 b) Abundance and functional states of immune cells using multiparameter flow cytometry

vector_chunks.text:
Summary

STINGa as a single agent does not induce significant death in cancer cells

Combination of STINGa with ATRi is effective in inducing cancer cell-specific death with no significant cytotoxic effect on immune cells

Higher does of STINGa decrease the proportion of T cells in some samples

Responding samples show higher proportion of CD8+ T cells compared to non-responders

Discussion points

Publication plans: manuscript review and acknowledgements
Preparation of the final report: specific guidelines and formats
How to deal with the left over compound

Re analysis of the samples treated in 96 wells or Shandon slides

To set the threshold for markers, CellProfiler analysis was re done for most of the samples:
Integrated intensity for CK7, and mean intensity for CD45 /gH2AX were displayed from nuclear / cell segmentation
Intensity from the cells (CK7, and CD45), and nuclei (Gh2ax) were plotted
Marker intensities varied across the samples, so threshold for each sample was different

Integrated intensity CK7 from Cell segmentation

Samples are stained with CK7 (tumor cell), CD45 (immune cells), gH2AX (DNA damage)
 and dead cell marker /cleaved caspase 3

The dead cell marker was not consistent across the samples, hence decided to analyze abundance of CK7 / CD45+ cells

Mean intensity CD45 from Cell segmentation

Challenges (1/3)

Boxplots for 96w and Slide samples (S071_pOme, S085_pOme, S102_pOme, S119_pOme, S114_pOme, S038_rAdn, S041_rOva, S045_rOva (treatment resistant))
PDS samples lacked reliable DCM (excluded ‘alive’ ratios)

Treatment resistant sample plots did not match Elina’s plots (Tried to replicate Elina’s code, spoke to Matilda and Jie about Elina’s filtering approach, and decided to stick with our script and Ashwini reanalyzed S038_rAdn and S045_rOva)

HRD vs HRP boxplots: S119 is seemingly sensitivity to Ola and Pembro despite HRP status (S119_pOme: Of the 10 images, in 9 images Cell Profiler recognized fewer than 2 CK7 cells despite observable tumor spheres. Perhaps this discrepancy is due to a staining error, for example, the Ab was not evenly distributed or images were over exposed)
Should we exclude S119?

vector_chunks.text:
Challenges (2/3)

Boxplots, heatmaps, and combined heatmaps for 384w samples (S141_pOval, S147_pOvaR, S156_AdnR, S203_pOme, S245_pAdn, S250_pOme, S255_pOme, S270_pAdn, S290_pOme)
Results from the plots did not accurately represent abundance of cells in images (Ashwini re-set thresholds for each sample and created a spreadsheet with thresholds per patient → automatically loaded into script)

Handling Na values and Inf (I went through each sample to determine the reason for NaNa or Inf values. Calculating ratio produced NA values if there are 0 cells, of a certain criteria, in the treatment group wells. Inf values are produced if the DMSO group wells contain 0 cells. For this reason, in the heatmaps I set Na values to the lowest fold change value and Inf to the highest fold change value so that this data can still be plotted)

Which ratios are the most meaningful? (Review dot heatmaps together)

Looks like many cells died in the DMSO wells in sample S141, S147, and both S156 samples (Unfortunately these samples are mostly HRD. Perhaps we can divide or subtract by the DMSO proportion?)

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: Optimization of the HGSOC Tumor Tissue Dissociation Protocol

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: Aim: To optimize the protocol for HGSOC tissue dissociation to obtain most viable tumor and immune cell populations.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: Background: Tissue dissociation protocol vary depending on the stiffness, cellularity and nature of the tissue type. Major factors affecting cell viability, stress response and cellular compositions are nature of the tissue dissociating enzymes, temperature and the incubation time (Flanagan et al., 2019, Adam et al., 2017). In addition, buffers and media used in the protocol might also have an effect on the cell viability and the composition. Kopper er al., used collagenase for ovarian cancer tissue dissociation to establish organoid cultures. However, Flanagan et al., show that compared to dissociation with cold active protease at 60C, digestion with collagenase at 370C results in activation of stress response in all the tissue types. Furthermore, cold active protease yielded enhanced capture of T cells and NK cells. Here we test different enzymes, and dissociation temperatures on the HGOC tissue and evaluate the final yield, cell viability and the cell types using immunofluorescence (IF).

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: Protocol:

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: 1. Dissection:

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: 1.1. Place the tumor tissue in a 10 cm tissue culture dish inside the laminar hood. Cut the tissue into three pieces (two small and one large piece) using a sterile scalpel and or scissors. Place one of the small piece into a pre-labelled eppendorf tube and snap freeze in liquid nitrogen for DNA/RNA isolation. Place another small piece into a pre-labelled histo cassettes and fix the tissue in 4% formaldehyde (in 1X PBS) overnight at 4 0C. Use the remaining bigger piece for enzymatic dissociation and single cell isolation.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: 2. Enzymatic dissociation:

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: 2.1. In the laminar hood, place the big tumor piece in a 10 cm tissue culture dish and mince into smaller pieces using a sterile scalpel and or scissors. Add couple of drops of AdDF+++ while mincing so that tissue does not dry out.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: 2.2. Divide the minced tumor tissue into three tubes and perform the enzymatic digestion as follows

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: Tube 1: 1 mg/ml Collagenase in AdDF+++ with 5uM Y-27632, incubate at @ 370C for 1 hr while keeping the tubes on a shaker.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: Tube 2: 1 U/ml Dispase in AdDF+++ with 5uM Y-27632, incubate at @ 370C for 1 hr while keeping the tubes on a shaker.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: Tube 3: 10 mg/ ml Subtilsin A in DPBS+5mM Cacl2+ 5uM Y-27632 @ 60C for 30 min while keeping the tubes on a shaker.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: 3. Filtration and centrifugation

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: 3.1. Pre-wet 70µm strainer with cold AdDF+++ medium +5uM Y-27632+50U DNase, or DPBS+5mM Cacl2+ 5uM Y-27632+50U DNase. Pipette the digested suspensions through the strainer into 50mL tubes and wash the strainers with 5 mL above cold medium.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: 3.2. Centrifuge 10 minutes @ 1000 rpm 4oC.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: 3.3. Repeat the step 2.2. for the tissue fragments retained on top of the cell strainer.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: 4. Red blood cell lysis (ONLY if there is a visible red pellet)

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: 4.1. Remove supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 2 mL RBC lysis buffer, incubate at room temperature (RT) for 5 min. Add 10 ml AdDF+++ Centrifuge 10 minutes @ 1000 rpm 4oC.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: 5. Resuspension of the cell pellet for downstream analysis and cultivation

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: 5.1. Carefully remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in AdDF+++ medium. Perform and cell count and determine total cell yield.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: 5.2. Pipette 5ul of concentrated cell suspension on top of a Poly-D-Lysine hydrobromide (PLD) coated slides. Air-dry horizontally 5-10 min until cells do not move (do not over-dry!) Fix 10min @ 40C with PFA and wash with PBS. Proceed with the IF.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Patient_tissues_FRESH/Optimization_of_the_HGSOC_Tumor_Tissue_Dissociation_Protocol.docx
vector_chunks.text: 5.3. Determine the cell concentration required for organoid culture and proceed with the cultivation.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx

vector_chunks.text: 4% Formaldehyde: 5ml of 37-41% Formaldehyde + 45 ml 1x PBS, located in the chemical cabinet next to fume hood

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: Collagenase and Hyaluronidase: 10x, dilute to 1x (located in Färkkilä lab -200C enzyme box)

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: Dispase II: stock 50U/mL, dilute to 1U/mL: 20 ul per ml (located in Färkkilä lab +40C PDL box) if you run out of this ask from Kauppi lab

vector_chunks.text: Subtilsin A: stock 100mg/mL, dilute to 10mg/mL: 100 ul per ml (located in Färkkilä lab -200C enzyme box)

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: RQ1-RNAse free DNAs: 50 units (50 ul) per reaction (located in Färkkilä lab -200C enzyme box)

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: AdDF+++ medium: DMEM/F12 (500mL) + 1x Glutamax (5mL of 100x)+ 10mM HEPES (5mL 1M)+ Penstrep (5mL): Ready to use media will be in Väharautio lab cell culture fridge.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: PBS+5mM Cacl2: 1xPBS (100 ml bottle) & 50mM CaCl2 (50 ml falcon tube) kept in the basket in Väharautio lab cell culture.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: Start date:

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: Protocol:

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: Sample ID:

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: Brief description of the sample and its appearance.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: 1. Dissection:

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: 1.1. Cut the tumor tissue into three pieces (two small and one large). Snap freeze one of the small tumor piece into LN2, fix the remaining one small piece into 4% formaldehyde (in 1X PBS) overnight at 4 0C. Use the remaining bigger piece for enzymatic dissociation and single cell isolation.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: 2. Enzymatic dissociation:

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: 2.1. In the laminar hood, place the big tumor piece in a 10 cm tissue culture dish and mince into smaller pieces using a sterile scalpel and or scissors

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: 2.2. Divide the minced tumor tissue into three 3 ml tubes and perform the enzymatic digestion as follows

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: Tube 1: 3 ml of 1 mg/ml Collagenase (300 ul of 10x in 3 mL of media) in AdDF+++ with 50U DNase I (50 ul), incubate at @ 370C for 1 hr shake the tubes manually every 15-20 min.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: Tube 2: 3 ml of 1 U/ml Dispase (60 uL of Dispase stock in 3 mL mecdia) in AdDF+++ with 50U DNase I (50 ul) incubate at @ 370C for 1 hr shake the tubes manually every 15-20 min.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: Tube 3: 10 mg/ ml Subtilsin A (300 ul of stock in 3 mL of media) in PBS+5mM Cacl2+ 50U DNase I (50 ul) @ 60C (inside the cold centrifuge) shake the tubes manually every 15-20 min, incubate for 1 hr.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: Note: Total volume of the dissociation varies depending on the quantity of the tumor tissue. Use 1-2 ml, or 3-5 ml for smaller and bigger tissues, respectively.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: 3. Filtration and centrifugation

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: 3.1. Pre-wet 70µm strainer with cold AdDF+++ medium. Pipette the digested suspensions through the strainer into 50mL tubes and wash the strainers with 5 mL above cold medium.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: 3.2. Centrifuge 10 minutes @ 1000 rpm 4oC.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: Note: Aliquot the medium and sterile PBS into a falcon and keep it on ice. Always use cold medium or PBS until the final step.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: 4. Red blood cell lysis (ONLY if there is a visible red pellet)

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: 4.1. Upon centrifugation from step 3.2., if there is a visible red pellet, remove supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 2 mL RBC lysis buffer, incubate at room temperature (RT) for 5 min. Add 10 ml AdDF+++ Centrifuge 10 minutes @ 1000 rpm 4oC.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: 5. Resuspension of the cell pellet viabililty measurement and downstream analysis

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: 5.1. Carefully remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 1mL of sterile 1x PBS. Perform and cell count and determine total cell yield.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: Note: Decide the volume of resuspension depending on the pellet size.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: Viability analysis using Trypan Blue:

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: Take 10uL of cell suspension in a 1.5 ml tube add 10 ul of trypan blue mix well. Pipette into cell counting chamber. Note down the cell count & viability.

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: Cell count using countess

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/Protocol_for_HGSOC_tissue_dissociation.docx
vector_chunks.text: After the cell count, add 2ml of cold PBS, centrifuge at 1000 rpm for 10 min @ +4. Divide the pellet into three tubes: 1. Approx. 500000 cells for IF and viability analysis, 2. & 3. For single cell analysis (EPE) and for organoid culture (AN). Place the tubes in a cell freezing box and keep it at -80.

vector_chunks.text:
Cell Freezing: 
For optimum results, cells for cryopreservation sho uld be in log phase of growth. Similar or standard 
freezing protocols may be substituted. 
1. Examine and make sure the cell culture is free of c ontamination, in healthy situation and proper 
confluency, etc. 
2. Perform a cell count to determine the viability of cells 
3. Gently pellet the cells by centrifugation (3 - 5 mi nutes at 1,000~2,000rpm, 4 oC). Remove the 
supernatant by using an aspirator. 
4. Gently suspend the cells with STEM-CELLBANKER ® cryopreservation medium (1 ml for 5 x 10 5 - 5 x 10 6 
cells). 
5. Dispense the cell suspension in 1ml aliquots to cry opreservation vials that have been labeled with the 
cell line name, cell concentration, passage date an d other essential information. 
6. Place the vials directly in a -80 oC for storage. If necessary, transfer the frozen vi als to a liquid nitrogen 
storage tank after the vials have been frozen for a t least 24 hours. 
7. Optimum protocol may change with the cell types. 
 
 
 
 
 STEM -CELLBANKER ® DMSO 
FREE 
Cryopreservation Medium 
• Serum-Free 
• Chemically Defined 
• GMP Manufactured 
 
 
Cat#: 11897F 
Qty: 20ml 
Expiry Date: 3 years from manufacturing date (see l abel)

vector_chunks.source_file: 8_iPDC_1.0/ORGANOID cultures/Stem-CellBanker.pdf
vector_chunks.text:
Thawing: 
1. Remove the frozen cell from storage and quickly tha w in a 37 oC shaking water bath. 
2. Immediately dilute and gently mix each 1ml of cells with 10ml of complete cell culture medium. 
3. Gently pellet the cells by centrifugation (3-5 minu tes at 1,000 - 2,000rpm, 4 oC). Remove the supernatant 
by aspirator. 
4. Gently suspend the cells with appropriate volume of complete cell culture medium and plate in a cultur e 
flask. 
5. Continue the further culture procedures according t o standard protocols. 
Guarantee of Quality: 
1. Manufactured in compliance with JPN, EU, US, and PI C/S GMP guidelines 
2. Bacterial contamination free – Product has been tes ted and confirmed to be free of bacteria, fungi and 
mycoplasma. 
3. Chemical Analysis: pH (7.0 to 8.5 at room temperatu re) Endotoxin (below <5 EU/mL) 
4. Performance test – Cell viability above 80% (JM404, SK-007) is guaranteed. 
Storage of STEM-CELLBANKER ®: 
1. STEM-CELLBANKER ® should be stored at 4 oC or below. 
2. Keep frozen in case of long term unused period (3 m onths or longer) 
3. Repeated freezing and thawing may impair the qualit y of the product, it is recommended that STEM-
CELLBANKER ® is aliquoted before freezing.
